docs.title: COMPASS Documentation
docs.text: COMPASS (Crew Operations Management & Personnel Assignment Support System) is the official platform for STARFLEET International chapter management in Region 3 and beyond.

docs.title: Where do you want to start?
docs.text:
• CO Quick Start — New to COMPASS? Get your ship set up.
  • Roster Guide — Add crew, manage transfers, update ranks.
  • Promotions Guide — Submit promotions and track requirements.
  • Events Guide — Log meetings and track attendance.

docs.title: What is COMPASS?
docs.text:
COMPASS replaces the legacy Access database with a modern, multi-ship platform giving COs real-time visibility into crew activity and promotion progress. It manages crew rosters, promotions, events, awards, academy records, and announcements.

COMPASS is a supplemental platform — it works alongside the SFI Members Portal (db.sfi.org), not as a replacement.

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text: Check the relevant guide's Common Issues section, or contact your Regional Coordinator.

docs.title: Commanding Officer Guide
docs.text: This guide covers everything a CO (or XO) needs to run their ship effectively in COMPASS.

docs.title: In This Guide
docs.text: Section What You'll Learn Getting Started Ship setup, importing your roster, first login Command Dashboard Your operational hub — pending actions, crew overview, quick links Managing Your Roster Adding, editing, transferring crew; roster import; departments Promotions Eligibility checking, submitting promotions, the regional approval workflow Events Creating events, recording attendance, activity tracking Awards & Nominations Nominating crew, award categories, the nomination workflow Reports Crew activity, promotion points, MSR, membership expiration, and more Ship Settings Point values, promotion requirements, Discord integration

docs.title: Your Role in COMPASS
docs.text:
As CO, you are the primary administrator for your ship's data. Your XO shares most permissions and can act on your behalf for all day-to-day operations.

Keep It Current

COMPASS is most useful when data is entered promptly. A roster updated weekly takes about 5 minutes. A roster updated quarterly takes hours to reconcile.

docs.title: Awards & Nominations
docs.text:
COMPASS manages the full awards workflow from CO nominations through regional approval and permanent point awards.

Go to Awards in the left navigation.

docs.title: Nominating a Crew Member
docs.text:
Go to Awards → Nominate (or click Awards & Recognition from the Command Dashboard).

The nomination form requires:

  • Nominee — Search by name or SCC number. Check Nominee is not in COMPASS if nominating a member from outside the platform.
  • Award Type — Filter the award catalog by category (Order of Merit, Annual Awards, Auxiliary Awards, Regional Awards, Ship Awards)
  • Award — Select the specific award from the filtered list
  • Citation — A narrative description of why the crew member deserves this award (minimum 50 characters, up to 3000)
  • Supporting Evidence — Optional additional details, links, or documentation

Write a Good Citation

Be specific. "For organizing three consecutive monthly meetings during the CO's LOA, maintaining 90% crew attendance" is far stronger than "for outstanding service." The RC uses your citation when reviewing the nomination — a vague citation slows approval.

docs.title: After Submitting
docs.text:
Once submitted, your nomination is visible on the Award Nominations page with a status badge.

Nominations move through these statuses:

Status Meaning Pending CO Review Just submitted, awaiting your own review queue (for crew-submitted nominations) In Progress Under regional review Approved Approved — permanent points awarded automatically Rejected Not approved — a reason is provided

docs.title: Award Categories
docs.text:
Category Examples Order of Merit SFI's primary award series — multiple classes and grades Annual Awards SFI annual cycle awards (CO of the Year, etc.) Auxiliary Awards SFMC and other auxiliary-specific awards Regional Awards Awards created and managed at the regional level Ship Awards Awards created by your ship's command staff

Each award's point value is applied automatically to the member's permanent points upon approval.

docs.title: Permanent Points
docs.text: Approved awards add to a member's permanent point total, which contributes to promotion eligibility. You can see a member's full permanent points breakdown by going to Permanent Points in the left navigation.

docs.title: Annual Awards
docs.text: The SFI annual award cycle is managed separately from regular nominations. Nominations are submitted during a specific window announced by your RC. EC-level annual awards are voted on by the Executive Committee. Your RC will announce when the window opens — watch for that communication.

docs.title: RC-Level Award Workflow
docs.text: For reference, once you submit a nomination your RC sees it in their regional awards queue for review and approval. The RC can approve directly or request changes.

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
Award not in the catalog. Contact your RC — the award catalog is managed at the regional and fleet level. Custom ship awards can be added by your RC on request.

Points didn't update after approval. Refresh the member's profile. If still missing, contact the platform admin.

I submitted with the wrong nominee. Contact your RC before it's approved — they can return it for correction.

docs.title: Command Dashboard
docs.text: The Command Dashboard is your primary hub in COMPASS. It's the first page you see after logging in as CO or XO, and gives you a full operational picture of your ship at a glance.

docs.title: Crew Management
docs.text:
Quick links for the most common roster operations:

Link Purpose Ship Information View and edit your ship's details Departments Manage department assignments Roster Import Import or re-sync your roster from the SFI database SFI Database Sync Sync crew eligibility data from db.sfi.org SFDPP Training View privacy training compliance

docs.title: Advancement & Recognition
docs.text: Link Purpose Promotions Review and submit promotion requests — shows a badge count for pending items Awards & Recognition Nominate crew and manage award submissions

docs.title: Operations
docs.text: Link Purpose Events Calendar Create events and manage attendance

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Link Purpose Ship Settings Point values, promotion requirements, SFMC integration Quick Links Customize the links shown on the crew member dashboard API Tokens Manage external integration tokens Discord Bot Configure Discord role sync, nicknames, and sync settings

docs.title: Pending Actions
docs.text: Shows promotion and award submissions that require your attention, with a direct action button for each. If you have pending items, this panel will always be visible on the dashboard. Clearing your pending actions regularly keeps the workflow moving for your crew.

docs.title: SFDPP Compliance
docs.text:
Shows whether all role holders (CO, XO, and department heads) have completed current SFDPP privacy training. Displays a compliance percentage and a View all link to see the full breakdown.

Tip

SFDPP compliance is required for command roles. If this shows less than 100%, follow up with the listed crew members before your next report cycle.

docs.title: Crew by Department
docs.text: A horizontal bar chart showing how many crew are assigned to each department. Also shows the count of crew not yet assigned to any department — a useful indicator that your roster needs attention.

docs.title: Recent Activity
docs.text: The bottom of the dashboard shows a timestamped feed of recent platform activity — profile updates, new crew additions, event entries, and more. Useful for keeping tabs on what's been entered (or not entered) without digging into individual records.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
The left sidebar provides access to all COMPASS sections. The top of the sidebar shows your ship name and your current role. As CO or XO, you'll see all sections. Crew members see a reduced set.

Note

If you hold both a ship-level role (CO/XO) and a regional role (RC/VRC), you'll see both the Command Dashboard and Regional Dashboard in the navigation.

docs.title: Events
docs.text:
Event tracking builds the activity record that drives promotion eligibility, award nominations, and monthly reports. Every meeting, activity, and convention appearance should be logged.

Go to Events in the left navigation.

docs.title: Creating an Event
docs.text:
Click Create Event from the Events page.

Required fields:

  • Event Name — A descriptive title (e.g., "June Monthly Meeting", "Regional Summit 2026")
  • Event Type — Determines point value (see below)
  • Date — The date the event occurred
  • Attending Crew — Check each member who attended

docs.title: Event Types
docs.text:
Type Examples Monthly Meeting Regular ship meetings Ship Activity Social events, watch parties, game nights, group outings Convention Regional Summit, local or national cons Community Service Charity work, outreach, public service activities Training / Academy Study groups, test prep sessions

Point values per event type are configured per ship in Ship Settings → Event Points. Your RC may set regional defaults that apply automatically.

docs.title: Recording Attendance
docs.text:
Check each crew member who attended when creating the event. You can edit attendance after the fact by opening the event and clicking Edit Attendance.

Tip

Enter attendance the same day or the next morning. Remembering who attended two weeks later is much harder — and missing attendance means missing points toward promotion eligibility.

docs.title: Editing or Deleting an Event
docs.text:
Open the event from the Events list and click Edit to change any details or update the attendance list. Events can be deleted if entered in error, but deletion is permanent — there's no undo.

Warning

Deleting an event removes all attendance records and the associated points for all attendees. If the event was just entered with the wrong date or wrong type, edit it rather than deleting and re-entering.

docs.title: How Events Drive Promotions
docs.text: Each attended event awards the attending crew member a number of activity points, based on the event type's configured point value. These points accumulate toward promotion eligibility requirements. You can see a member's point total and the events driving it on their profile under the Promotions tab.

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
I forgot to log an event from last month. Enter it with the correct past date — no problem. COMPASS doesn't restrict event entry by date.

A member's attendance isn't showing in their promotion record. Open the event and verify they're checked in the attendance list. Also check that the event type has a non-zero point value configured in Ship Settings.

I entered the wrong event type and points were awarded incorrectly. Edit the event and change the type. Points will recalculate automatically.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks you through setting up your ship in COMPASS for the first time. The process takes about five minutes and is largely automatic — COMPASS pulls your ship data and crew roster directly from the SFI Members Portal.

docs.title: Before You Begin
docs.text:
You'll need:

  • Your db.sfi.org username and password (your SFI database credentials — not a COMPASS password)
  • To be listed as CO or XO of your chapter in db.sfi.org — setup will fail if you're not

These are your SFI credentials, not a COMPASS account

COMPASS uses your db.sfi.org credentials once to pull your chapter info and roster. They are never stored. If you don't have db.sfi.org credentials, log in at db.sfi.org first to set them up.

docs.title: Step 1 — Enter Your SFI Credentials
docs.text:
Go to compass.sfi.org and click Add Your Ship to COMPASS on the login page.

Enter your db.sfi.org username and password and click Connect to SFI. COMPASS authenticates against the SFI database and fetches your chapter information and roster. A loading spinner will appear while it works.

Only CO or XO can run setup

COMPASS verifies your role against db.sfi.org. If you're not listed as CO or XO, you'll see an error: "Only the Commanding Officer or Executive Officer can set up a ship in COMPASS." Have your CO or XO run setup instead.

Common errors at this step:

  • "Unable to authenticate" — double-check your db.sfi.org username and password. Log in at db.sfi.org directly to confirm they work.
  • "Unable to retrieve chapter information" — your account authenticated but has no CO/XO role on file. Contact your RC.

docs.title: Step 2 — Select Your Chapter (if prompted)
docs.text: If your SFI account is associated with more than one chapter, COMPASS will show a chapter selection screen. Pick the chapter you're setting up and continue. If you only have one chapter, this step is skipped automatically.

docs.title: Step 3 — Review Your Ship Information
docs.text:
COMPASS displays a Review & Confirm page showing everything pulled from db.sfi.org.

The page shows:

  • Ship Information — name, registry, class, region, type, commissioned date, charter city
  • Command Staff — CO and XO with SCCs; your name will have a You badge
  • Crew Roster Preview — every member COMPASS found in your db.sfi.org roster, with position, rank, name, and SCC

Review this carefully. If the ship name, registry, or command staff looks wrong, click ← Start Over and contact your RC — the data comes directly from db.sfi.org, so any errors need to be corrected there first.

Ship already exists in COMPASS

If your ship has already been set up, you'll see an orange warning banner and the Create Ship button won't appear. Contact your RC to get access to the existing record.

docs.title: Step 4 — Create Ship & Import Crew
docs.text:
If everything looks correct, click Create Ship & Import Crew.

COMPASS will:

  1. Create your ship record — name, registry, class, region, and all chapter details
  2. Import your entire crew roster — all members from db.sfi.org are added with their ranks, positions, and SCCs
  3. Create your COMPASS account — you're automatically logged in after setup
  4. Sync your eligibility data — OTS/OCC completions and academy records are pulled from db.sfi.org

Don't close the page

A loading spinner appears while provisioning runs. This can take 10–15 seconds depending on roster size. Don't close or refresh the page while it's working.

docs.title: Step 5 — You're In
docs.text: On success, COMPASS redirects you to the Command Dashboard with a confirmation message. Your ship is live with your full roster already imported.

docs.title: After Setup — What to Do Next
Set Your Password
docs.text: Your COMPASS account was created automatically. Go to Profile → Change Password and set a permanent password before logging out.

docs.title: Verify Your Roster
docs.text: Go to Crew and scan through your imported roster. Check that all active members are present and ranks look correct. If someone is missing, it means they weren't on your chapter's roster in db.sfi.org.

docs.title: Invite Your XO
docs.text: Your XO was imported as a crew member but may not have a COMPASS account yet. Direct them to compass.sfi.org to register — they'll need to use the same email or SCC on file. Their command permissions apply automatically once their account is active.

docs.title: Log Your First Event
docs.text: Go to Events → Create Event and enter your most recent ship meeting. See the Events Guide.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Setup failed partway through. The setup runs as a single transaction — if anything fails, nothing is saved. You can safely run it again from the start.

My roster shows 0 members. Your chapter may not have a roster on file in db.sfi.org. Your ship record will still be created — add crew manually via Crew → Add Member.

I already have a COMPASS account. That's fine — COMPASS detects your existing account by SCC and links it to the new ship rather than creating a duplicate.

My ship was previously in COMPASS but was withdrawn. COMPASS reactivates it automatically if your registry matches a withdrawn ship. Historical data (awards, promotion history, events) is fully preserved. Crew members will need to rejoin through the normal join request process.

docs.title: Promotions
docs.text:
COMPASS manages the full promotion workflow from eligibility checking through approval and SFI database update. This page covers the complete process for both standard promotions (O-1 through O-5) and flag officer promotions (O-6 and above).

Go to Promotions in the left navigation to open the Promotion Eligibility Report.

docs.title: The Promotion Eligibility Report
docs.text:
The report has three tabs:

Tab What It Shows Eligibility All crew with current eligibility status, points, and time-in-grade Pending Promotions submitted and awaiting regional review History All past promotions for your ship

docs.title: Standard Promotions (E-1 through O-5)
docs.text: These promotions cover all Cadet (C-1 through C-6), Enlisted (E-1 through E-9), and junior Officer (O-1 through O-5) ranks. They are approved directly by the ship CO in COMPASS — no regional review required.

docs.title: Step 1 — Check Eligibility
docs.text: On the Eligibility tab, each crew member shows their current rank, time-in-grade, point totals, and eligibility status. A Promote button appears on rows where all requirements are met.

docs.title: Step 2 — Open the Promotion Form
docs.text:
Click Promote to open the promotion dialog for that crew member.

The form shows:

  • Available Points — Event + Academy points and Awards + Permanent points broken out separately
  • Promote To — Target rank, pre-selected as the next eligible rank
  • Points to Spend — How many Event + Academy points will be consumed by this promotion
  • Requirements — All requirements with green checkmarks confirming they're met
  • Promotion Date — Defaults to today; can be set to any past or future date
  • Effective Time — Optional; leave blank to apply at the start of the promotion date
  • TIG Waiver — Reduces time-in-grade requirement by 50% for extraordinary service
  • Notes — Optional internal notes visible to the RC

docs.title: Step 3 — Approve
docs.text: Click Approve Now. The promotion is applied immediately — no regional review required. The member's rank updates in COMPASS and they receive an email notification.

docs.title: O-6 Promotions (Captain)
docs.text: Promotion to Captain (O-6) requires RC approval. The form works the same as standard promotions but the submission routes to the RC rather than approving immediately.

docs.title: Step 1 — Open the Promotion Form
docs.text: Click Promote on the eligible crew member. The O-6 form looks similar to a standard promotion.

docs.title: Step 2 — Submit for RC Approval
docs.text: Click Approve Now to submit. The promotion routes to the RC's regional queue rather than approving immediately. The member is notified and the promotion appears in your Pending tab.

docs.title: Flag Officer Promotions (O-7 and above)
docs.text: Promotions to Fleet Captain (O-7) and above follow an extended workflow with additional narrative questions and SFI Fleet Command involvement.

docs.title: Step 1 — Open the Promotion Form
docs.text:
The form opens the same way but shows additional O-7+ content at the bottom.

Notice the differences from a standard promotion:

  • Points to Spend is 0 — permanent points drive eligibility, not event points
  • The TIG Waiver description changes to "Extraordinary Service – reduces TIG by 6 months"
  • An O-6+ Promotion – Commanding Officer Recommendation section appears at the bottom

docs.title: Step 2 — Complete the CO Recommendation
docs.text:
Scroll down to the CO Recommendation section. This is required for all O-7+ promotions.

First, select your recommendation:

  • ✓ Recommend Approval — You support this promotion
  • ✗ Don't Recommend Approval — You do not support it (rare, but available)

Then complete the Flag Promotion Nomination Form Questions. These answers are pre-filled onto the official SFI Flag Promotion Nomination Form PDF and forwarded to the RC:

Question What to Include Q1. Nominator/Nominee Relationship Your role relative to the nominee (e.g., Commanding Officer) Q2. Why should this person be promoted? Their overall service record and qualities Q3. Specific examples benefiting their Chapter Concrete contributions to their ship Q4. Specific examples benefiting their Region Regional service, events, leadership Q5. Specific examples benefiting STARFLEET Fleet-level contributions Q7. Outside hobbies or activities Community activities relevant to their character Q8. Other awards or documentation Any additional supporting evidence

Write Substantive Answers

Flag promotion narratives are reviewed by your RC and by SFI Fleet Command. Vague answers delay approval. Write specific, concrete examples with dates where possible. The RC can edit answers inline before forwarding, but strong original submissions move faster.

Click Submit for RC Approval when complete.

docs.title: RC Review — O-6 Promotions
docs.text: Once submitted, the promotion appears in the RC's Regional Promotions queue under O-6 Pending Approval.

docs.title: RC Reviews the Submission
docs.text:
The RC opens the review panel showing the full submission — member details and nomination form answers.

The RC has three options:

  • Approve Promotion — Approves directly; rank updates in COMPASS immediately
  • Reject Promotion — Rejects with a reason sent back to the CO
  • Cancel — Closes without action

docs.title: RC Review — O-7+ Promotions
docs.text: For O-7 (Fleet Captain) and above, the RC cannot approve directly — these require SFI Fleet Command approval. The RC's role is to review, endorse, and forward the nomination.

docs.title: RC Reviews and Recommends to Fleet
docs.text:
The RC reviews the submission the same way as O-6, but the action buttons at the bottom are different.

The RC has three options:

  • Recommend to Fleet — Forwards the nomination to SFI Fleet Command with optional endorsement notes
  • Nomination Form — Downloads the pre-filled Flag Promotion Nomination Form PDF
  • Reject — Rejects the nomination at the regional level

docs.title: RC Recommends to Fleet
docs.text:
Clicking Recommend to Fleet opens a confirmation dialog where the RC can add optional recommendation notes to Fleet Command.

After clicking Recommend to Fleet, COMPASS returns to the Regional Promotions page with a confirmation banner. The promotion moves from the O-7+ Pending Recommendation queue into the Recently Actioned table with a status of Awaiting Fleet Decision.

From this view the RC can see:

  • The green confirmation banner confirming the nomination was sent to Fleet Command
  • Any remaining O-7+ promotions still needing action in the O-7+ Pending Recommendation queue
  • The Recently Actioned table showing the promotion with Awaiting Fleet Decision status
  • Two action buttons: Record Fleet Decision (for when Fleet responds) and Nomination Form (to download the PDF at any time)

The external approval process now continues outside COMPASS. SFI Fleet Command will review the nomination and respond. When they do, the RC returns here to record the outcome.

docs.title: The Flag Promotion Nomination Form PDF
docs.text:
At any point during review, the RC can download the pre-filled SFI Flag Promotion Nomination Form by clicking Nomination Form.

COMPASS pre-fills the entire form from data already in the system:

  • Nominee name, SCC, join date, email, region, and chapter
  • Chapter CO name and email
  • Current rank and rank date
  • Requested rank
  • All relevant positions held since last rank date (pulled from ship assignments)
  • OTS, OCC, and current SFDPP certification dates

This eliminates manual form-filling. The RC downloads and submits this PDF to SFI Membership Services as part of the external O-7+ approval process.

docs.title: Recording the Fleet Decision
docs.text:
Once SFI Fleet Command responds, the RC records the outcome back in COMPASS using the Record Fleet Decision button on the promotion row.

Select Approved or Rejected, add any notes from Fleet Command, and click Submit Decision. If approved, the member's rank updates in COMPASS and they are notified.

docs.title: Approval Workflow Summary
docs.text: Rank Approving Authority COMPASS Action Cadet (C-1 through C-6) Ship CO CO approves directly in COMPASS Enlisted (E-1 through E-9) Ship CO CO approves directly in COMPASS O-1 through O-5 Ship CO CO approves directly in COMPASS O-6 (Captain) RC RC approves directly in COMPASS O-7 (Fleet Captain) SFI Fleet Command RC recommends to Fleet; RC records decision O-8 (Commodore) SFI Fleet Command RC recommends to Fleet; RC records decision O-9 (Rear Admiral) SFI Fleet Command RC recommends to Fleet; RC records decision O-10 (Vice Admiral) Executive Committee RC recommends to Fleet; EC vote required O-11+ (Admiral and above) Executive Committee RC recommends to Fleet; EC vote required

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
Member shows ineligible but should qualify. Check that all event attendance, academy completions, and awards are entered — missing data is the most common cause. Also confirm their OTS/OCC status is current via SFI Database Sync.

I need to correct a submission already sent to the RC. Contact your RC — they can edit narrative answers inline or return the submission for correction before approving.

The Flag Promotion PDF is missing positions held. Positions are pulled from COMPASS ship assignment records. If a position isn't showing, check that it's recorded in Ship Positions with the correct date range.

Status shows "Awaiting Fleet Decision" but Fleet has responded. The RC needs to record the fleet decision in COMPASS using the Record Fleet Decision button on that promotion row.

docs.title: Reports
docs.text:
COMPASS includes a full suite of reports for monitoring your ship's health, tracking crew activity, and generating required SFI submissions.

Go to Command Dashboard → Reports to reach the Reports & Analytics page.

docs.title: Crew Activity Report
docs.text:
Shows event participation rates, attendance streaks, active crew tracking, and an Inactive Crew Alert highlighting members who haven't attended any events in the selected period.

Use this report monthly to identify members who may be slipping into inactivity before they actually leave.

docs.title: Event Statistics Report
docs.text: Breaks down your events by type, attendance trends, and most-attended events over a selectable time period.

docs.title: Promotion Points Report
docs.text: Shows a detailed points breakdown for all crew members — event points, academy points, permanent points, and total — to help determine promotion eligibility at a glance.

docs.title: Points Accumulation Report
docs.text: Tracks recognition and promotion point trends across your crew over time.

docs.title: Academy Completion Report
docs.text: Shows OTS, OCC, and academy course completions by crew member. Useful for identifying who needs training to qualify for command roles.

docs.title: Membership Expiration Report
docs.text: Tracks crew members with memberships expiring in the next 60, 45, 30, 21, 14, or 7 days, and those already expired. Run this monthly before submitting reports to avoid losing crew to lapsed memberships.

docs.title: Community Service Hours Report
docs.text: Tracks volunteer hours from community service event types across your crew.

docs.title: Uniform Compliance Report
docs.text: Tracks uniform wearing rates and bonus points earned for crew who wear their uniform to events.

docs.title: Crew Platform Engagement
docs.text: Shows which crew members are actively logging into COMPASS and identifies dormant accounts — useful for following up with members who may not know the platform exists.

docs.title: Monthly Status Report (MSR)
docs.text:
Generates the MSR for SFI submission, listing current and previous month activity with participant counts.

Tip

Run the MSR report at the end of each month, review for accuracy, and submit to your RC. The RC manual requires monthly submission — COMPASS does the counting for you, but you still need to formally submit.

docs.title: Individual Crew Report
docs.text: A complete profile report for a specific crew member. Access this from the crew roster by clicking a member's name rather than from the main Reports page.

docs.title: Exporting Data
docs.text:
The bottom of the Reports page has three export buttons:

Export Contents Export Crew Roster Full crew list with ranks, statuses, and SCCs Export Events All events with dates, types, and attendance counts Export Participation Data Per-member event attendance and points data

Exports are CSV format, suitable for Excel or Google Sheets.

docs.title: Managing Your Roster
docs.text:
Your crew roster is the foundation of everything in COMPASS — promotion eligibility, event attendance, awards, and reports all depend on an accurate roster.

Go to Crew Roster in the left navigation to view your full ship roster.

docs.title: Member Statuses
docs.text:
Status Meaning Active Full member, counts toward all reports and promotion tracking Reserve Limited participation, still on roster LOA Leave of Absence — temporarily inactive, intends to return Inactive No current participation Transferred Moved to another ship Retired Left SFI or the chapter

Tip

Use LOA for members who plan to return. Inactive is for members who have stopped participating but haven't formally left. Avoid using Retired unless the member has actually departed — it removes them from active reporting.

docs.title: Adding a Crew Member
docs.text:
Go to Crew Roster → Add Member. Required fields: SCC, full name, rank, and status. Recommended: email address and join date.

Duplicate SCCs

COMPASS won't allow two members with the same SCC on the same ship. If you get a duplicate error, check whether they're already on your roster under a slightly different name, or contact your RC if they appear on another ship.

docs.title: Editing a Crew Member
docs.text: Click any member's name to open their profile, then click Edit to update their information. From a member's profile you can also update their rank, change their status, manage department assignments, and view their promotion and awards history.

docs.title: Importing Your Roster from SFI
docs.text:
If your ship's roster in db.sfi.org has changed — new members joined, ranks updated — you can re-sync directly from the Command Dashboard.

Go to Command Dashboard → Roster Import.

Click Sync from SFI Database and enter your db.sfi.org credentials when prompted. COMPASS will fetch your latest roster, detect changes (new members, rank changes, departures), and walk you through reviewing those changes before applying the import.

If the automatic sync isn't available, use Manual Import to paste CSV data from the SFI roster export page.

Note

Credentials entered here are used once and never stored — the same as the initial ship setup.

docs.title: Transferring a Member
docs.text:
1. Open the member's profile
  2. Go to Actions → Transfer Member
  3. Select the destination ship and confirm

The member will show as pending on the receiving ship until that ship's CO confirms the transfer. Inter-region transfers require RC approval from both regions.

docs.title: Ship Positions
docs.text:
Go to Ship Positions in the left navigation to view and manage formal position assignments (CO, XO, Department Heads, etc.).

To assign or change a position, go to Ship Positions → Manage Positions.

docs.title: Departments
docs.text:
Go to Departments from the Command Dashboard or left navigation to manage your ship's department structure and assign crew.

Crew not assigned to a department appear in the Unassigned count on the Command Dashboard. Assigning crew to departments keeps your org chart accurate and helps with department-level reporting.

docs.title: Roster Best Practices
docs.text:
• Audit monthly before submitting reports
  • Enter new members promptly — don't wait until report time
  • Use LOA for members who intend to return, not Inactive
  • Never delete members — change their status instead; historical data is preserved

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
I added a member but they can't log in. They need to register at compass.sfi.org separately. Their SCC will link their account to your ship automatically.

A member appears on two ships. Contact your RC to resolve. Crew can only have one active assignment at a time.

Roster import shows a member with the wrong rank. db.sfi.org is the source of truth for rank. If the rank is wrong in SFI's database, it needs to be corrected there first, then re-imported.

docs.title: Ship Settings
docs.text:
Ship Settings controls how COMPASS calculates points and promotion requirements for your ship. All settings here only affect your ship — other ships continue using their own values.

Go to Command Dashboard → Ship Settings.

docs.title: Ship Information
docs.text:
View and edit your ship's basic details — name, registry, class, region, charter city, chapter contact email, and website.

Go to Command Dashboard → Ship Information to update these.

docs.title: Event Points
docs.text:
Configure how many activity points each event type awards to attending crew.

Go to Ship Settings → Event Points to set your values. Each event type has a default SFI value shown alongside your ship's current setting. Click Set Override to customize.

Note

If your ship is using default values, point calculations automatically follow any regional defaults your RC has configured. Setting a ship-level override locks your ship to your chosen value regardless of regional changes.

docs.title: Academy Points
docs.text:
Configure point values for academy course completions by grade.

Grade Default Distinction 2 pts Honors 1 pt Pass 1 pt

Override these if your ship uses a different point scale for academy completions.

docs.title: Award Points
docs.text:
Set the permanent point values awarded when a crew member receives each award. Organized by award category (Orders of Merit, Annual Awards, Regional Awards, Ship Awards, Auxiliary Awards).

Each award shows the default SFI point value and your ship's current override. Click Set Override to customize any award's point value for your ship.

docs.title: Promotion Requirements
docs.text:
Configure the point and time-in-grade requirements for each rank promotion.

For each rank, you can set:

  • Points Required — Minimum points to be eligible
  • Time in Grade — Minimum months at current rank before promotion

Warning

Changes to promotion requirements take effect immediately and recalculate all crew eligibility. Review the Promotion Eligibility Report after making changes to see the impact.

docs.title: Permanent Award Points
docs.text: Configure how permanent point bonuses are applied for specific award types and leadership positions.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
Customize the quick-action links that appear on crew member dashboards. This is how you surface the most relevant resources for your crew — your ship's website, meeting schedule, Discord server, etc.

Go to Command Dashboard → Quick Links to manage these.

docs.title: Discord Bot
docs.text:
If your ship uses a Discord server, COMPASS can sync roles, update member nicknames, and keep your Discord membership list in sync with your COMPASS roster.

Go to Command Dashboard → Discord Bot to configure the integration.

docs.title: API Tokens
docs.text:
For ships or region staff using external integrations, API tokens can be generated here.

Go to Command Dashboard → API Tokens to manage tokens.

docs.title: Crew Member Guide
docs.text: This guide is for crew members — anyone who is not the CO or XO of their ship.

docs.title: Logging In
docs.text:
Go to compass.sfi.org and log in with your COMPASS username and password.

First time logging in?

Your CO sets up your ship in COMPASS and imports the crew roster. Once imported, you can register at compass.sfi.org using your SCC number to link your account to your ship. If you're not sure whether your ship is in COMPASS, ask your CO.

If you've forgotten your password, click Forgot password? on the login page to reset it by email.

docs.title: Your Dashboard
docs.text:
After logging in, you'll see your Crew Dashboard — a personal summary of your activity in COMPASS.

  • Your rank and ship — Current rank, position, and ship assignment
  • Recent events — Events you've attended recently
  • Promotion progress — Your current point total and how far you are from your next eligible promotion
  • Recent awards — Awards you've received
  • Quick links — Links your CO has configured for your ship (website, Discord, meeting schedule, etc.)

docs.title: Your Profile
docs.text:
Go to Profile in the left navigation to view and edit your personal information.

From your profile you can:

  • Update your contact information and email address
  • Change your password
  • View your complete rank history
  • View your awards history
  • View your academy completions and permanent points

Tip

Keep your email address current — COMPASS sends notifications for promotions, awards, and membership expiration reminders to the address on your profile.

docs.title: Viewing Your Promotion Progress
docs.text:
Go to Profile → Promotions Tab (or check your dashboard summary) to see:

  • Your current rank and time-in-grade
  • Your current point total broken down by category
  • Whether you currently meet the requirements for your next rank
  • What's outstanding if you don't yet qualify

docs.title: Academy Records
docs.text: Go to Academy in the left navigation to view your academy course completion history, including OTS, OCC, and individual course results.

docs.title: Permanent Points
docs.text: Go to Permanent Points in the left navigation to see your full permanent points breakdown — awards, leadership positions, OTS/OCC completions, and other sources.

docs.title: Nominating Someone for an Award
docs.text:
Crew members can submit award nominations. Go to Awards → Nominate and fill out the nomination form. Your nomination goes to the CO for review before moving to regional approval.

See the Awards guide for details on how to write a strong citation.

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
I can't see my ship's data. Make sure your account was registered with the correct SCC. If your SCC doesn't match what your CO imported, contact your CO to reconcile.

My rank in COMPASS is wrong. COMPASS pulls rank from db.sfi.org. If your rank is incorrect, it needs to be corrected in the SFI database first, then your CO can re-sync the roster.

I'm not receiving email notifications. Check that your profile email address is correct and check your spam folder. Notifications are sent from COMPASS automatically for promotions, awards, and membership reminders.

docs.title: Regional Coordinator Guide
docs.text:
This guide covers the COMPASS tools available to Regional Coordinators (RCs) and Vice Regional Coordinators (VRCs).

As RC, you have regional-level access across all ships in your region. Your COMPASS view includes a Regional Dashboard in addition to any ship-level Command Dashboard you may have as a CO.

docs.title: In This Guide
docs.text: Section What You'll Learn Regional Dashboard Monitoring region-wide ship health and pending actions Promotions Reviewing and approving promotion submissions from all ships Awards Reviewing and approving award nominations from all ships SFI Eligibility Sync Syncing crew eligibility data from db.sfi.org

docs.title: Your Role in COMPASS
docs.text:
As RC, you are the approving authority for promotions and awards across all ships in your region. You also have visibility into ship health indicators, membership expiration, and SFDPP compliance across your entire region — not just a single ship.

Note

If you are also a CO, you will see both Command Dashboard and Regional Dashboard in your navigation. They are separate views — the Command Dashboard shows your ship only; the Regional Dashboard shows all ships in your region.

docs.title: Awards (RC)
docs.text: As RC, you are the approving authority for all award nominations submitted by ships in your region. This page covers the full regional awards review workflow.

docs.title: The Awards Queue
docs.text:
Go to Awards in the regional navigation to see all pending award nominations from ships in your region.

Each nomination shows the nominee, award, nominator, ship, citation, and submission date. Nominations are grouped by award category.

docs.title: Reviewing a Nomination
docs.text:
Click on any nomination to open the full review panel. The panel shows:

  • Nominator Information — name, SCC, email, and their relationship to the nominee
  • Award Information — the specific award requested and its class/level
  • Basis for Recommendation — the nominator's citation narrative
  • RC Endorsement field — your optional endorsement statement

docs.title: Adding an RC Endorsement
docs.text: For Order of Merit nominations, you can add an RC Endorsement — a brief statement from you supporting the nomination. This is included in the official submission to SFI and strengthens the case for approval. While optional, it's good practice to add one for any nomination you're forwarding.

docs.title: Submitting to SFI
docs.text:
Once reviewed and endorsed, COMPASS provides three submission options at the bottom of the review panel:

Option Description Download OoM Form (PDF) Downloads the pre-filled SFI Order of Merit nomination form Submit to SFI Web Form Opens the SFI web form pre-filled with all nomination data Mark as Submitted to SFI Records that you've submitted externally (for your own tracking)

docs.title: The Pre-filled Nomination Form PDF
docs.text:
The generated PDF is pre-filled with all nominee, nominator, region, and chapter information — eliminating most of the manual data entry on the SFI side.

Review the PDF before submitting to confirm all details are correct. If anything needs correcting, return to the nomination and edit before downloading again.

docs.title: Notification Preferences
docs.text:
When approving award nominations, set the Notification Preference for how and when the member is told about their award:

Option When to Use Notify immediately Member can be told right away Hold until presented You plan to present the award at a meeting, event, or convention and want to announce it in person first

This setting is per nomination — you can hold some awards and immediately notify others based on the circumstances.

docs.title: Awards Workflow Tips
docs.text:
• Check the Pending Actions panel on your Regional Dashboard daily — it surfaces all award nominations waiting for your review alongside promotions
  • Always add an RC Endorsement for Order of Merit nominations — it strengthens the submission and signals to SFI that the nomination has regional backing
  • Use Hold until presented for awards you plan to announce at the Regional Summit or ship events — it makes for a better experience for the recipient
  • Encourage COs to write detailed citation narratives — vague citations slow down SFI processing and reflect poorly on the submission
  • The Mark as Submitted to SFI option is useful if you submitted via a method outside COMPASS (email, web form manually) — keep it checked off so your queue stays accurate

docs.title: Promotions (RC)
docs.text: As RC, you are the approving authority for all promotion submissions from ships in your region. This page covers the full regional review workflow for both standard and flag officer promotions.

docs.title: How Submissions Reach You
docs.text: When a CO submits a promotion in COMPASS, the member's row on their Promotion Eligibility Report changes to show Submitted for Approval with a Pending badge. The submission simultaneously appears in your Regional Promotions queue.

docs.title: The Regional Promotions Page
docs.text:
Go to Regional Promotions in the left navigation (visible only with an RC-level role) to see all pending submissions across your region.

The page is divided into three sections:

Section Contents O-6 Pending Approval Captain promotions awaiting your direct approval O-7+ Pending Recommendation Fleet Captain and above awaiting your recommendation to SFI Fleet Command Recently Actioned All promotions you've recently approved, rejected, or forwarded — with current status

Each row shows the member, ship, from/to rank, submitted by, submitted date, CO Recommendation status, and a Review button.

docs.title: Reviewing an O-6 Promotion (Captain)
docs.text:
Click Review on any O-6 submission to open the review panel.

The panel shows:

  • Member and ship details — name, ship, from/to rank, points, promotion date, submitted by
  • CO Recommendation — green badge if the CO recommends approval
  • Nomination Form Answers — the CO's narrative responses to all promotion questions

You can edit individual answers inline by clicking Edit on any question before approving — useful for correcting typos or clarifying answers before the record is finalised.

docs.title: Approving
docs.text: Click Approve Promotion. The member's rank updates in COMPASS immediately and they receive an email notification. The promotion moves to Recently Actioned with an Approved status badge.

docs.title: Rejecting
docs.text: Click Reject Promotion and provide a reason. The CO receives the rejection with your reason. The promotion is removed from the queue and the CO can correct and resubmit.

docs.title: Reviewing an O-7+ Promotion (Fleet Captain and above)
docs.text:
O-7+ promotions cannot be approved at the regional level — your role is to review, endorse, and forward to SFI Fleet Command. Click Review on any O-7+ submission.

Review the full submission including all nomination form answers. Edit any answers inline if needed.

You have three options:

Button Action Recommend to Fleet Forwards the nomination to SFI Fleet Command with optional endorsement notes Nomination Form Downloads the pre-filled Flag Promotion Nomination Form PDF Reject Rejects at the regional level — CO is notified with your reason

docs.title: Downloading the Nomination Form PDF
docs.text:
Before forwarding, download and review the generated PDF using the Nomination Form button. COMPASS pre-fills the entire form from data already in the system.

The PDF includes nominee details, current/requested rank, all positions held since last rank date, OTS/OCC/SFDPP completion dates, and the CO's narrative recommendation answers. Review it carefully — this is the document SFI Membership Services receives.

docs.title: Recommending to Fleet
docs.text:
Click Recommend to Fleet. A confirmation dialog appears where you can add optional notes to Fleet Command.

Click Recommend to Fleet to confirm. COMPASS returns to the Regional Promotions page with a confirmation banner.

docs.title: After Recommending — Awaiting Fleet Decision
docs.text:
After forwarding, the promotion moves to the Recently Actioned table with a status of Awaiting Fleet Decision. Two action buttons remain available.

Note

The external approval process now continues outside COMPASS. SFI Fleet Command will review the nomination and respond to you directly. Submit the PDF to SFI Membership Services per the standard process if you haven't already.

docs.title: Recording the Fleet Decision
docs.text:
When SFI Fleet Command responds, return to the Regional Promotions page and click Record Fleet Decision on the promotion row.

Select Approved or Rejected, add any notes from Fleet Command, and click Submit Decision.

If approved, the member's rank updates in COMPASS immediately and they receive an email notification. The promotion status changes to Approved in the Recently Actioned table.

docs.title: Promotion Workflow Tips
docs.text:
• Check the Pending Actions panel on your Regional Dashboard daily — it surfaces all promotions waiting for your review across all ships
  • Run SFI Eligibility Sync on a ship before processing a batch of their promotions to ensure OTS/OCC data is current
  • Download and review the Flag Promotion PDF before forwarding to Fleet — easier to catch data issues in the PDF view than in the form
  • Edit nomination narrative answers inline before approving if the CO's responses are vague — stronger narratives mean faster SFI processing
  • For O-7+ promotions, record the Fleet decision in COMPASS as soon as you hear back — it keeps your Recently Actioned table accurate and notifies the member promptly

docs.title: Regional Dashboard
docs.text:
The Regional Dashboard gives you a bird's-eye view of every ship in your region — health indicators, pending actions, membership status, and SFDPP compliance across your entire fleet.

Go to Regional Dashboard in the left navigation. This option only appears if you hold a regional role (RC, VRC, RCOS, or RAO).

docs.title: Ship Health Overview
docs.text:
A card or table for each ship in your region showing:

  • Ship name and registry
  • Active crew count
  • Recent event activity
  • Pending promotions or awards requiring your attention
  • SFDPP compliance status
  • Any ships flagged as needing attention

Ships with pending items show a badge count so you know at a glance where action is needed.

docs.title: Pending Actions
docs.text: A consolidated queue of all promotions and award nominations from every ship in your region that are waiting for your review. This is your primary daily working view as RC — clear this queue regularly to keep workflow moving for your COs.

docs.title: Region-Wide Membership Expiration
docs.text: An overview of membership expiration across all ships — who is expiring soon, region-wide. Use this to prompt COs to follow up with members before they lapse.

docs.title: SFDPP Compliance
docs.text: A region-wide view of SFDPP compliance for all role holders across all ships. Any ship with non-compliant command staff will be flagged here.

docs.title: Navigation in Regional View
docs.text:
When you have both a ship CO role and an RC role, the left navigation shows both Command Dashboard and Regional Dashboard. These are completely separate views:

  • Command Dashboard → your ship only (USS Veritas)
  • Regional Dashboard → all ships in Region 3

The ship displayed in the sidebar header reflects whichever context you're currently working in. You can switch between them freely without losing your place.

docs.title: Region 3 Ships
docs.text: Ship Registry Class USS Veritas NCC-74100 Sovereign Class USS Comanche NCC-1799 Constitution Class USS Corsair NCC-26556 Ambassador Class USS Zavala NCC-74709 Intrepid Class USS Joan of Arc NCC-73289 Entente Class USS Battle Born NCC-69607 Nebula Class

docs.title: SFI Eligibility Sync
docs.text:
The SFI Eligibility Sync pulls current membership status, OTS/OCC completions, academy records, and SFI affiliations directly from db.sfi.org for every crew member on a ship. This keeps COMPASS promotion eligibility calculations accurate without requiring manual data entry.

Go to Command Dashboard → SFI Database Sync for a single ship, or access it per-ship from the Regional Dashboard.

docs.title: What Gets Synced
docs.text:
Each sync pulls the following from db.sfi.org for every crew member:

Field Description SFI Status Active, expired, or suspended membership status OTS Officer Training School completion OCC Officer Candidate Course completion SFDPP Privacy training compliance status Courses Academy course completions and grades Affiliations SFI chapter affiliations on record

The SFI Eligibility Overview shows a summary count across all these fields for the ship.

docs.title: Running a Sync
docs.text:
From the SFI Eligibility Overview page, click Sync All Crew in the top right corner.

Enter your db.sfi.org credentials when prompted and click Login & Sync. COMPASS will authenticate against db.sfi.org and begin pulling eligibility data for each crew member.

The sync runs crew by crew and shows real-time progress. When complete, a summary banner shows how many records were synced, skipped, or failed.

A result of "X synced, 0 failed, Y skipped" is normal — skipped means the member's data was already up to date and no changes were needed.

docs.title: Reading the Eligibility Overview
docs.text:
The overview table shows each crew member with columns for:

Column Meaning SCC Member number Status SFI membership status (Active/Expired) OTS ✓ if completed, ✗ if not OCC ✓ if completed, ✗ if not SFDPP ✓ current, ⚠ expiring, ✗ not completed Courses Count of academy courses completed Affiliations SFI chapter affiliations on file Synced How recently data was pulled from db.sfi.org

Members with warnings or missing requirements will stand out visually — use this view to identify who needs to complete training before their next promotion.

docs.title: How Often to Sync
docs.text:
Run a sync at least monthly — ideally before processing any promotions or submitting regional reports. Since OTS and OCC completions in db.sfi.org are what gate officer promotions, stale data here is the most common cause of "member shows ineligible but should qualify" issues.

Tip

If a crew member just completed OTS or OCC, run an individual sync for that member immediately rather than waiting for the monthly sync. The sync is fast for a single member.

docs.title: Individual Member Sync
docs.text: To sync a single crew member rather than the whole ship, click the sync icon in the Actions column next to their row on the eligibility overview page. This is faster for spot-checking after a member completes training.

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
Sync failed for a specific member. The most common cause is a SCC mismatch — the SCC in COMPASS doesn't match what's on file in db.sfi.org. Check the member's profile and correct the SCC, then retry.

Member shows OTS incomplete but they finished it. Run an individual sync for that member to pull the latest data. If it still shows incomplete after syncing, the completion may not yet be recorded in db.sfi.org — the member should contact their academy administrator.

Sync completed but SFDPP still shows expired. SFDPP expiration is date-based. If the member completed recertification recently, confirm the completion date in db.sfi.org and sync again.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
CO — Commanding Officer. The ship's captain and primary COMPASS administrator.

COMPASS — Crew Operations Management & Personnel Assignment Support System.

db.sfi.org — The SFI Members Portal. COMPASS supplements but does not replace it.

EC — Executive Committee. SFI's governing body at the fleet level.

LOA — Leave of Absence. A temporary inactive status for members who intend to return.

OCC — Officer Candidate Course. An academy track for officer advancement.

OTS — Officer Training School. Entry-level officer training tracked in permanent points.

Permanent Points — Cumulative point total driving promotion eligibility. Earned through events, awards, academy completions, and leadership positions.

RC — Regional Coordinator. The regional administrator responsible for a group of ships.

RCOS — Regional Chief of Staff. Assists the RC in regional operations.

Region 3 — The SFI region including USS Veritas, USS Comanche, USS Corsair, USS Zavala, USS Joan of Arc, and USS Battle Born.

SCC — Star Cluster Certification (member number). Unique identifier for every SFI member.

SFI — STARFLEET International. The fan organization COMPASS serves.

SFMC — Star Fleet Marine Corps. SFI's marine auxiliary, managed through a dedicated COMPASS module.

XO — Executive Officer. The ship's first officer; shares most CO permissions in COMPASS.

docs.title: Navy Officer Ranks
docs.text: Grade Abbreviation Rank O-1 ENS Ensign O-2 LTJG Lieutenant Junior Grade O-3 LT Lieutenant O-4 LCDR Lieutenant Commander O-5 CMDR Commander O-6 CAPT Captain O-7 FCAPT Fleet Captain O-8 COMM Commodore O-9 RADM Rear Admiral O-10 VADM Vice Admiral O-11 ADM Admiral O-12 FADM Fleet Admiral

docs.title: Navy Enlisted Ranks
docs.text: Grade Abbreviation Rank E-1 CRMN 3C Crewman Third Class E-1 CRR Crewman Recruit E-2 CRMN 2C Crewman Second Class E-2 CRA Crewman Apprentice E-3 CRMN Crewman E-3 CRMN 1C Crewman First Class E-4 PO3 Petty Officer Third Class E-5 PO2 Petty Officer Second Class E-6 PO1 Petty Officer First Class E-7 CPO Chief Petty Officer E-8 SCPO Senior Chief Petty Officer E-9 MCPO Master Chief Petty Officer

docs.title: Cadet Ranks
docs.text: Grade Abbreviation C-1 CDT C-2 CDT4 C-3 CDT 3C C-4 CDT 2C C-5 CDT 1C C-6 CDT CRMN

docs.title: Marine Officer Ranks
docs.text: Grade Abbreviation Rank O-1 2LT Second Lieutenant O-2 1LT First Lieutenant O-3 MCPT Marine Captain O-4 MAJ Major O-5 LTC Lieutenant Colonel O-6 COL Colonel O-7 DBR Brigadier O-8 BGN Brigadier General O-9 MGN Major General O-10 LGN Lieutenant General O-11 GEN General

docs.title: Promotion Notes
docs.text:
• O-1 through O-6 — Standard ship/regional workflow, RC approves in COMPASS
  • O-7 through O-9 — Require SFI Fleet Command approval; RC recommends to Fleet in COMPASS
  • O-10 and above — Require Executive Committee vote; RC recommends to Fleet in COMPASS

Note

For the authoritative rank structure, refer to the SFI Member Handbook.
